page: Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, designed specifically for memory-intensive workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with a massive 336.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 945 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core throughput. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing and single-threaded gzip compression, supported by a strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate. However, due to its low core count of 4 vCPUs, multi-core performance is relatively limited, as evidenced by weak multi-core stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block speed, but a weak large-block read speed.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX16-4mds_v2 offers a highly specialized resource profile, prioritizing extreme memory capacity and high single-core clock speeds over parallel processing power. This represents a clear tradeoff: while it is inefficient for highly parallelized compute tasks or heavy multi-threaded compiling, it is exceptionally well-suited for memory-bound applications that do not require massive core counts. This server is optimized for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, text processing, and low-concurrency, high-memory enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmdsv2 family, running on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 physical cores and 4 vCPUs clocked at 4.2 GHz, paired with an exceptionally large 336.0 GB of RAM and 945 GB of local storage. Benchmarks reveal outstanding single-core performance, with top-tier scores in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, though multi-core performance remains weak due to the low core count. This resource profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive, low-concurrency applications rather than parallel compute tasks. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching systems, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 vCPUs, 336 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 336 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive server combining high single-core clock speeds with massive RAM capacity for specialized database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
Massive 336.0 GB RAM with 84.0 GB per core ratio
945 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
Optimized for in-memory databases and caching layers
